campbell.title: Campbell City Council — July 7, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council held a public hearing on July 7, 2026, to consider revoking business licenses and tobacco retailer permits for two smoke shops in the city. The council also discussed adopting a new interim ordinance related to Senate legislation and approved consultant service agreements and a grant submission for public works.
keyTopics: Revoking business licenses for California Cigar and Hayes Smoke Shop
New interim ordinance superseding previous Senate-related regulations
Public Works Director authorized to submit grant application
Approving consultant service agreements

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council approved an engineer's report for the fiscal year and scheduled an ordinance for adoption at their May 20 meeting. The council also discussed whether Saratoga should become a charter city and reviewed preliminary estimates of potential new tax options and revenue sources.
keyTopics: Engineer's Report approval for FY 2026
Ordinance adoption scheduled for May 20, 2026 meeting
Charter city status consideration
Preliminary tax options and revenue estimates

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve financial reports, authorize several construction and professional service contracts, and hear public appeals following a court order. The council reviewed the town's finances for February and approved contracts related to infrastructure improvements.
keyTopics: Approved February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Approved three-year professional services agreement
Public hearing on appeals following court order
Infrastructure improvements resolution

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on June 23, 2026, to discuss several local issues including whether to protect a historic building at 647 South Sixth Street, a proposal to allow ranked choice voting for filling vacant council or mayor seats, airport advertising contracts, and a long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy. Residents had the opportunity to submit written comments and speak during the meeting.
keyTopics: Historic landmark protection and Mills Act contract for 647 South Sixth Street property
Ranked choice voting ballot measure for filling mayor and council vacancies
Large format advertising sign contract at San José Mineta International Airport
Long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy North America

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a closed-door meeting on June 23, 2026, to discuss a new residential development project planned for 333 Franklin Street, hire a new City Attorney, and address an ongoing lawsuit involving water quality issues. Most of the substantive discussions happened in closed session, so specific details about the development project and legal matters weren't made public.
keyTopics: New residential development planned for 333 Franklin Street
Hiring a new City Attorney
Ongoing lawsuit: San Francisco Baykeeper v. City of Mountain View (water/environmental case in federal court)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-door meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting. The specific details of what was discussed in the closed session were not publicly disclosed, as is typical for private council sessions.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held
Regular council meeting convened
Flag salute ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a study session to review and update the Health and Safety section of the city's long-range General Plan covering 2015-2040. The council also recognized the Parks and Recreation Department for their work across multiple divisions and celebrated Monta Vista High School's Science Olympiad team achievement.
keyTopics: Updating health and safety policies in the city's 25-year General Plan
Recognition of Parks and Recreation Department divisions including youth programs, senior wellness, and sports facilities
Monta Vista High School Science Olympiad Team achievement recognition

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — July 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on July 14, 2026, available both in-person and online via Zoom. Residents can participate remotely or submit written comments before the meeting through the city's online system.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format allows in-person and remote participation
Zoom access available for residents unable to attend in-person
Written public comments can be submitted via eComment before the meeting
City Council meetings continue with remote participation options

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review updates to the city's 2025-2030 housing and community development plan, consider changes to how council vacancies are filled, and handle routine administrative items like approving previous meeting minutes and previewing upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Update to 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan (federal housing and community development funding)
Changes to council vacancy ordinance procedures
Councilmembers' requests for new workplan items
Approval of previous meeting minutes and council calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council honored Bill Gutoff's retirement and held public hearings on utility rate changes for water, wastewater, and trash services in fiscal year 2027. The Finance Committee recommended approving new rate schedules and financial plans for these three utilities.
keyTopics: Water utility rates and reserve management for 2027
Wastewater collection rates and financial forecast
Trash/refuse utility rate adjustments
Retirement recognition for Bill Gutoff
